channel.title: SEAtoday - Learning & Education

Monday, March 16, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Rockler Woodworking and Hardware, 832 NE Northgate Way, Seattle, WA

Salt Cellar class

Event details

item.title: Inside the Clerkship
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Sullivan Hall, 110, Seattle, WA

Join us for an exclusive and candid panel discussion featuring distinguished judges and former and current law clerks who will demystify the clerkship application process. Designed for law students at all stages, this event offers firsthand insight into what judges look for in applicants, how to stand out in a competitive pool, and how to strategically prepare throughout law school. Panelists will share practical advice on building strong relationships with faculty, crafting compelling application materials, navigating interviews, and positioning yourself for both state and federal clerkships. Attendees will also gain valuable perspective on what life is like inside chambers and how a clerkship can shape long-term career trajectories. In addition, this program will preview the Federal Litigation course offered in Fall 2026. Attendees will learn how the course strengthens the research, writing, and advocacy skills that judges value most in clerkship candidates, and how it can position students for success in competitive judicial hiring. Whether you're just beginning to consider a clerkship or preparing to apply, this program will provide actionable guidance, insider tips, and an opportunity to ask questions directly to those involved in the hiring process—while offering a forward-looking glimpse at how the upcoming Federal Litigation course can support your goals. Panelists: Judge Roopali Desai, United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit Judge Kymberly Evanson, United States District Court for the Western District of Washington Judge Jamal Whitehead, United States District Court for the Western District of Washington Luther Reed-Caulkins, Current Judicial Law Clerk to the Honorable Kymberly Evanson Ruby Nagamine, Current Judicial Law Clerk to the Honorable Richard A. Jones, United States District Court for the Western District of Washington Dalvin Yarbrough, Former Judicial Law Clerk for The Honorable Reggie B. Walton, United States District Court for the District of Columbia, 2023-2025 Moderator: Cynthia Jones, Jones Legal Group and Adjunct Professor, Seattle University School of Law \(Federal Litigation\) Date: March 16th, 2026 Time: 4:00 PM to 5:30 PM Location: Sullivan Hall, Room 110

Event details

item.title: Marketing Mondays
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Collective Chemistry, 5201 11th Avenue Northwest, Seattle, WA

Sip and strategize in solidarity!

Event details

item.title: Food and Cooking Through Science
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Gain a deeper insight into what’s going on when you cook and bake! Whether you are a home cook or a professional, you can become a much better cook by learning the science of food and cooking. You'll fall in love with cooking, as well as the beauty of the amazing world we live in. Focus on understanding the fascinating field of food science through a combination of fun and fascinating lectures and demos. Learn to make better choices about cooking methods and techniques, and how to produce better finished products more easily and consistently. Elevate your knowledge about nutrition, how food works, sustainability issues, and the chemistry of cooking and baking. Develop modern techniques using scientific principles. Learn many fundamental concepts and apply them to real-world cooking and baking.

Event details

item.title: Bicycle Roadside Repair - Prepare for the Unexpected
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Shoreline Tool Library, 16610 Aurora Avenue North, Shoreline, WA

Hands-on class for on-the-go bike fixes. Learn to repair flats, broken spokes and more so you can keep riding no matter the distance.

Event details

item.title: ONLINE: Winter Creative Workspace 2026 - A 12
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Fremont Abbey Arts Center, 4272 Fremont Ave North, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: Smarty Pints! Tree Care and Big Data Astronomy
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Burke-Gilman Brewing Company, 3626 NE 45th St, Suite 102, Seattle, WA

Spring is coming, and with it the occasional appearance of that weird big blue cloud! On March 16 we’re looking towards the sky, and beyond, with talks on maple trees and care \(timely!\) and the search for life beyond Earth \(never not timely!\). See you at 6:30 in the taproom! 🌳 Zach McCurry ISA Certified Arborist, Emerald Arbor Care Arborist Tells All: The Secret Life of a Broadleaf Maple The secret life of trees revealed, the newest scientific field, and how it affects you in Seattle. Learn how an arborist sees your trees, how to care for them, & what good tree work is all about. 👽 Prof. James Davenport Research Assistant Professor, UW Astronomy Director, DiRAC Institute Searching for ET with ‘big data’ astronomy New wide field sky surveys, backed with powerful computational systems, allow us to start new and exciting ways of asking the age old question: Are we alone? Kick back, look up, and ponder the trees and the stars \(but recline after Smarty Pints, we need space for you to sit!\)

Event details

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

Event details

item.title: Coworking Tuesdays
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Bothell Kenmore Chamber, 10031 Main Street, Suite A, Bothell, WA

Tuesday Cowork \+ Coffee/Tea \(Weekly\) Need a reason to get out of your inbox cave and into a productive, low-pressure space? Join us every Tuesday for a drop-in coworking morning—bring your laptop, knock out your priorities, and enjoy the energy of working alongside other local professionals. When: Tuesdays | 9:30–11:30 AM | No registration needed Format: Come for the full two hours or pop in when you can Who it’s for: Business owners, remote workers, creatives, students, nonprofit leaders—anyone who wants focused work time \+ light community What to expect: Quiet “get-it-done” work blocks, optional hellos \+ quick intros, and a friendly vibe \(no forced networking\) Location \(through February\): Bothell Visitor Center We’ll rotate locations monthly—same time, same Tuesday rhythm, fresh spaces. Bring: Your laptop \+ whatever you need to work. We have the coffee, tea, and water! Questions? Email sandra@bothellkenmorechamber.org

Event details

item.title: Chamber Basics Workshop
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: West Seattle Chamber Office, 5450 California Ave SW, Seattle, WA

Description Make the most of your membership at this Membership Basics Workshop! Learn how to utilize the tools of your Chamber membership and get your business growing! Bring your laptop to follow along as go over how to update your profile, add events, and more at this how to workshop. This workshop is perfect for new members who want to learn what's at your disposal and existing members that want to enhance their membership!

Event details

item.title: Beeswax Botanical Image Transfer
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Ray Pfortner. In this hands-on workshop you learn everything you need to take your botanical art to the next level. Using 5″ x 7” photocopies of your own garden photos, illustrations, or journal pages, you will learn how to transfer your image onto wood panels, tiles, or stone using melted beeswax. You will bring your own photocopies; all additional equipment and materials will be provided. $31.50 BBGS Member/$45 Non-Member

Event details

item.title: City Chat
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: FROST, 15217 Main Street #106, Mill Creek, WA

City Chat presents an excellent opportunity for residents and business owners to meet and engage with our new Mayor Stephanie Vignal and Mayor Pro Tem Melissa Duque. Join fellow residents, ask questions, and share their thoughts and opinions. You'll also get updates on City services and learn about upcoming events. This is a great opportunity to share your concerns, suggestions and more! City Chat is open to the public, no RSVP is required. We hope to see you there!

Event details

item.title: Working Together to Protect and Transfer Assets - VIRTUAL WEBINAR
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 11 AM

Description ️ Working Together to Protect and Transfer Assets Presented by: Peggy Haslach & Patrice Kreider-Hughes Location: GSBA Office Date & Time: March 18 11am - 12:30pm Join GSBA for an informative session designed to help LGBTQ\+ individuals, couples, and families protect what matters most. Working Together to Protect and Transfer Assets brings together a financial planner and an estate planning attorney to show how collaboration ensures your wishes are honored, your assets are protected, and your loved ones are cared for. What You'll Learn How to build a trusted planning team that understands LGBTQ\+ financial and legal needs Strategies to protect assets during your lifetime with coordinated insurance, investments, and legal planning Essential documents: durable powers of attorney, healthcare directives, wills, and trusts Aligning beneficiary designations with legal structures for seamless asset transfer Best practices across diverse family structures and varying state laws Featured Speakers Peggy Haslach, CFP, CLU Financial Planner working with women professionals and small business owners Peggy built her practice serving women professionals \(including veterinarians, doctors, and attorneys\) and small business owners. After seeing first-hand how life events impact women's financial security, she pivoted careers at 53-focused on collaborative, educational planning for women and LGBTQ\+ clients. Her clients are forward-thinking women who've put the pieces together-business, life, goals, beliefs, and savings-but want a cohesive plan they fully understand. Outside of work, Peggy competes nationally in dog agility and shares that passion with her wife, Desiree, a national and world champion handler and trainer. They live in Sumner, Washington with their three Border Collies-Vista, Cru, and Peak. Patrice M. Kreider-Hughes, Esq. Founder & Principal, Kreider Hughes Law, PLLC Patrice is an estate planning and small-business attorney with 15\+ years of experience. She earned her B.A. in psychology from the University of Virginia and her J.D. from Seattle University School of Law. Before founding Kreider Hughes Law in 2017, she served as in-house counsel for Conover Insurance, handling mergers and acquisitions, contracts, and employment matters. Featured on the Seattle Connected podcast and recognized by Expertise.com in 2023 and 2024, Patrice lives in Seattle with her wife, their daughter Simone, and pets Luther and Emma. Session Outline Why Collaboration Matters Financial planners and estate attorneys working in tandem to build resilient, values-aligned plans. Building Your Planning Team Roles, responsibilities, and coordination between advisors who understand LGBTQ\+ needs. Protecting Assets During Your Lifetime Insurance \(life, disability, long-term care\), investment strategy \(diversification, ESG\), and ownership/titling alignment. Legal Documents That Safeguard Your Wishes Durable POA, healthcare POA & advance directive, wills & trusts, and syncing beneficiary designations. Coordinating Asset Transfers Real estate titling, retirement & insurance beneficiaries, business succession, and philanthropic giving. Navigating Legal Landscapes & Family Structures Marriage recognition, parental rights, cross-border/visa considerations, and state-by-state variability. Q&A \(15 min\) - Bring your questions on real estate, trusts, chosen family, and children's inheritance planning. Who Should Attend LGBTQ\+ individuals, couples, and families seeking long-term financial security Small business owners planning for succession and legacy Anyone looking to align legal and financial strategies

Event details

item.description:

item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Event details

item.title: UW Psychology Distinguished Alumni Lecture, Feat. Dr. Cynthia Berg
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: UW Hans Rosling Center for Population Health, 3980 15th Ave NE, Seattle, WA

University of Washington Department of Psychology - Distinguished Alumni Lecture, Feat. Dr. Cynthia Berg A Self- and Social-Regulation Perspective on Type 1 Diabetes Management Across the Life Span Type 1 diabetes is a difficult illness to manage and requires daily self-regulation \(e.g., planning to meet diabetes goals, self-efficacy, and managing emotions\) as well as social regulation \(disclosing to others to obtain needed assistance\). Although aspects of parent involvement in diabetes management decline across adolescence and into young adulthood, our longitudinal studies show that having family members and romantic partners who are knowledgeable about one’s diabetes behaviors and are available to help when needed is associated with better diabetes outcomes. Social regulation can also facilitate more effective use of technologies such as continuous glucose monitors by sharing GM data with others. These studies have served as the foundation for randomized clinical trials that hold promise for optimizing self- and social-regulation on a daily basis for individuals across the life span.

Event details

item.title: Small Business Meetup
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: The Cloud Room, 1424 11th Ave Suite 400, Seattle, WA

Are you a small business owner \(1-5 employees\) who has been in business less than 5 years? Would you like to meet with other small businesses, talk about best practices, share business wisdom, and build community? Please join fellow Cloud Room members in creating a small business circle to support each other! Reach out to Heidi or Angela with any questions or if you would like to join.

Event details

item.title: smART Ventures Information Session
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Event Types: Arts,Training-Classes/Workshops Neighborhoo ds: Citywide,Online Event Contact: smART Ventures Team Contact Email: smartventures@seattle.gov Audience: All Pre-Register: Yes Cost: Free Link: https://www.seattle.gov/arts/programs/grants/smart-ventures-grant After reviewing the smART Ventures guidelines, attend a group session to learn how to submit your best application. Sessions are available the 1st and 3rd Thursdays of each month \(excluding City Holidays\) Register here and you will receive a Teams Link in your confirmation email.

Event details

item.title: Immigration is Not Invasion: Dean's Luminaries in Law Series with Ilya Somin
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Sullivan Hall, C5, Seattle, WA

Seattle University School of Law’s Luminaries in Law Series continues with a timely and thought-provoking conversation titled Immigration Is Not Invasion, featuring renowned constitutional law scholar Ilya Somin. In this special lunchtime event, Professor Somin will be interviewed by Anthony E. Varona, Dean of Seattle University School of Law, and Andrew Siegel, Professor of Law. Together, they will explore Professor Somin’s scholarship challenging the increasingly common framing of immigration as an “invasion,” examining its constitutional, historical, and democratic implications. The discussion will engage questions at the intersection of immigration law, constitutional structure, political rhetoric, and civil liberties, offering insight into how legal doctrine and public discourse shape immigration policy and democratic values. Drawing on their respective expertise, Dean Varona and Professor Siegel will guide a wide-ranging conversation designed to illuminate one of the most pressing legal and societal debates of our time. Students, faculty, and members of the broader legal community are invited to attend this engaging installment of the Luminaries in Law series. Date: March 19th Location: Room C5, Sullivan Hall Time: 12:00 PM to 12:50 PM.

Event details

item.title: Free Virtual Sessions for Adoption
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Northwest Adoption Exchange \(NWAE\) is inviting all those interested in adopting from foster care to attend free, virtual information sessions to learn about the process. Offered in partnership with the Washington State Department of Children, Youth, and Families \(DCYF\), these sessions provide guidance and clarity for anyone beginning or continuing their adoption journey. Who: Open to anyone interested in adopting from foster care in Washington state. What: Free virtual information sessions on the adoption process. When: Monthly sessions, participants are encouraged to attend Session 1 before attending Session 2.  Upcoming dates: ·         Session 1: March 5 at 12 p.m. or 6 p.m. ·         Session 2: March 19 at 12 p.m. or 6 p.m. Where: Online, register at nwae.org Cost: Free Led by experienced child welfare professionals, NWAE’s sessions are presented virtually in two parts and offer a comprehensive introduction to adoption. Participants will gain insights into the needs of youth waiting for permanent families and learn how to navigate each stage of the adoption journey with confidence. Session 1: Introduction to adoption from foster care Explore how adoption fits into the foster care system, the types of support available, and what to consider when determining if adoption is right for your family. Session 2: The steps to adoption Walk through the practical aspects of adoption, from choosing an agency and completing required training to navigating the home study process.

Event details

item.title: Albers Graduate Information Session
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Interested in a graduate program at Albers? Have questions about the admissions process? Want to connect with a current student or faculty member from your program of interest? Our online information sessions provide more insight into your desired program and answer any questions you may have. Join us at one of our events to learn more! During our graduate information sessions we take a deep dive into the following programs offered by the Albers School of Business and Economics: Early Career MBAProfessional MBA \(in-person program only\)Sport and Entertainment Management MBAMaster of Professional Accounting \(STEM, for non-accounting majors\)Master of Science in Accounting & Analytics \(STEM, for accounting majors\)Master of Science in Business Analytics \(in-person program only\) \(STEM\)Master of Science in Finance \(STEM\) We provide detailed information such as program structure, prerequisites, application information, admissions processes, scholarships, and more. To participate with video, you will need access to a device with a camera \(laptop, smart phone, etc.\). A Zoom account is not required. You may also choose to participate through audio only. Albers has separate recruiting events for the following: Online: MBA, MSBA, Cybersecurity Leadership programs Leadership Executive programs including the MBA and Certificate Please email albersgradinfo@seattleu.edu for more information.

Event details

item.title: BOARD OF DIRECTORS MEETING
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 7:30 AM

The Chamber's Board of Directors meets monthly to review organizational business, financial reports, and strategic initiatives. These meetings provide an opportunity for the board to discuss ongoing programs, evaluate progress toward goals, and make decisions that guide the Chamber's operations and community impact. This is a regularly scheduled governance meeting of the Chamber's leadership team.

Event details

item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 8 AM

item.title: Moments with a Mentor
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Seattle Southside Chamber of Commerce, 3100 S 176th St Suite 210, SeaTac, WA

Moments with a Mentor Moments with a Mentor is a unique networking and professional development experience designed to connect emerging leaders with established professionals in an intimate, conversation-focused setting. This event creates space for meaningful dialogue, guidance, and inspiration as participants engage in small-group or one-on-one mentoring sessions. Attendees will have the opportunity to hear personal stories, gain practical career insights, and learn strategies for navigating professional challenges from leaders across a variety of industries. Whether you're seeking direction, building confidence, or expanding your network, Moments with a Mentor offers a supportive environment to grow and be inspired. Join us for an empowering experience that encourages connection, fosters mentorship, and strengthens our community's future leaders-one conversation at a time.

Event details

item.title: Community Health Gathering: Reducing Harm Through Poison Prevention
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Raise awareness about the risks of unintentional poisoning and promote safety strategies. State and local data sharing and reduce harm.

Friday, March 20, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Swoon City, 1716 Northwest Market Street, Seattle, WA

Get ready to stitch your way to stylish earrings with our Botanical Thread Painted Earrings embroidery class - creativity at its finest!

Event details

item.title: Women's Conference
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Join us for a day centered on Jesus, our wonderful counselor. We’ll hear from Kacey Batterton, a licensed counselor, sharing how the care Jesus modeled in scripture is echoed in how counselors are taught to care for others. Together, we’ll explore what Christlike empathy, listening, and compassion can look like for all of us. Not as experts, but as women learning to love well. This is a free, one-day event open to women of all ages \(teens 13\+ welcome\). Bring a friend, everyone is invited! Lunch isn’t included, but we’ll have a room reserved at a local restaurant for anyone who’d like to gather during the break. Come as you are. Be encouraged, equipped, and reminded of the gentle care of Jesus.

Event details

item.title: Nourishing Networks Spring 2026 NetWorkshop
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Together Center, 16307 NE 83rd St, Redmond, WA

Nourishers gather together! We're all needed now more than ever.

Event details

item.title: The Conflict Without Collapse™ Experience
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Metropolitan Park West Tower, 1761 Minor Avenue, Seattle, WA

A one-day immersive journey to transform conflict into connection through clarity, courage, and restoration.

item.title: Photography Basics for Nikon Users - LIVE
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Kenmore Camera, 6708 NE 181st St, Kenmore, WA

Class for new users of Nikon interchangeable lens cameras.

Event details

item.title: Best Day Ever: South Park
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: South Park Hall, 1253 S Cloverdale St., Seattle, WA

Join us for our first Best Day Ever event with Cultivate South Park! This exciting one-day event will feature activities like West African dance & storytelling, chocolate tasting, Duwamish River tours, local food tasting, arts & crafts, and more. Sign up now for a full day of joy and community! Best Day Ever is a one-day, community-powered experience that celebrates the vibrancy, culture, and pride of Seattle’s neighborhoods. Co-created by the Department of Neighborhoods \(DON\) and the Seattle Chamber of Connection \(SCoC\), Best Day Ever strengthens civic belonging by bringing people, place, and purpose together. Guided by DON’s Community Engagement Framework and the Chamber’s Six Points of Connection, this initiative uplifts community leadership and honors the histories, creativity, and cultural assets that make each neighborhood unique. A thriving neighborhood is one where residents feel connected, represented, and proud of where they live — and Best Day Ever is designed to bring that feeling to life. Each activation builds belonging through service, storytelling, movement, shared meals, joyful connection, and economic visibility for local small businesses.

Event details

item.title: Introduction to Pruning with Plant Amnesty
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Speaker: Kristin Jackson, Executive Director of Plant Amnesty. Presentation: Pruning makes sense when you understand the science behind it. You will be introduced to the basics of tree biology and structure, tree responses to the basic types of pruning cuts, pruning standards and, best management practice. Cost: $9.00 Register

Event details

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop [VIRTUAL]
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   Virtual workshops are held every 3rd and 4th Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. A Zoom link will be sent to you via a ULMS Housing department staff member upon completing registration.

Event details

item.title: BrainFest with Allen Institute
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pacific Science Center, 200 Second Ave. North, Seattle, WA

Join us for the third annual BrainFest, a day of brainy fun for all ages at PacSci. Explore hands-on activities, meet Allen Institute scientists and collaborators, learn about cutting-edge neuroscience research, and even touch a real brain! Whether you’re a budding young scientist, a lifelong learner, or simply fascinated by how the mind works, BrainFest 2026 promises a full day of discovery, inspiration, and wonder. Tickets BrainFest is included with General Admission. A limited number of free general admission tickets will be given out on the day of the event on a first-come first-serve basis. This event is a partnership of the Allen Institute and Pacific Science Center.

Event details

item.title: Home buyer class up to 150k in assistance
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Join our in-person Home Buyer Class and score up to $150k in home assistance—don’t miss out!

Event details

item.title: Chai Blending Workshop
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Friday Afternoon Tea, 4228 Stone Way North, Seattle, WA

Learn about the diverse and rich world of chai and the special bond a family chai recipe forges. Blend and take home your own house chai!

Event details

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

Event details

item.title: IMPROV 100 MONDAY - Intro to Improv: Say Yes!, SPRING'26
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Improv School 5700 6th Ave S Suite 109 Seattle 98108, 5700 6th Ave S Suite 109, Seattle, WA

Say yes! Learn to listen, connect, play and use your own life to create stories with others! An 8 week intro to improvised theatre!

item.title: Hyper-Local Happy Hour (Non-alcoholic)
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Calvary: The Hill, 1107 E Howell St, Seattle, WA

Get to know your hyper-local Capitol Hill neighbors around Cal Anderson Park in an open house style, non-alcoholic happy hour!  Enjoy non-alcoholic beers and yummy mocktails, along with a charcuterie spread, all inside a 120-year-old historic Capitol Hill church building. These happy hours will be from 4-5:30pm every 4th Tuesday through June  January 27th February 24th March 24th April 28th May 26th June 23rd \*Please note that ADA access is available into the happy hour area, but not to the restrooms unfortunately \(very old building!\)

Event details

item.title: Kirkland Business Pitch Competition 2026
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Kirkland Performance Center, 350 Kirkland Ave, Kirkland, WA

Join us for a transformative Pitch Competition at the Kirkland Performance Center, designed for business owners, property owners, community members, investors, and all who are interested in helping a business grow! Tickets: Registration attend as an audience member is requested but not required. Date: Pitch Competition: March 24, 5:30 PM Doors Open at Kirkland Performance Center Pitch Categories: People’s Choice Start-Up Expansion Prizes: Top finalists in each category win prizes of $5,000! Networking Opportunities: Connect with fellow entrepreneurs, industry experts, local mentors, and potential collaborators. Let’s grow, innovate, and collaborate! For more information, contact Christian Lash at Clash@kirklandwa.gov

Event details

item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

item.title: ONLINE: Winter Creative Workspace 2026 - A 12
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Fremont Abbey Arts Center, 4272 Fremont Ave North, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: IMPROV 100 TUESDAY - Intro to Improv: Say Yes!, SPRING'26
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Say yes! Learn to listen, connect, play and use your own life to create stories with others! An 8 week intro to improvised theatre! Discover Unexpected Productions' philosophy of improvisation, developed over our 43 years of improvising in Seattle! You'll learn how to actively listen, to use your own life to create collaborative stories, and you'll work on the fundamental principles of improvisation: what it means to say, "Yes, And", how to tell stories that include everyone in the room, how to stop worrying about failing, and the games and concepts behind what we do on stages around the world! Under the guidance of one of our best Seattle improv teachers, you will learn to:· -Be a Better Listener -Conquer Fears -Spark Creativity -Meet New People & Make New Friends -Become a Better Problem Solver -Gain Confidence· -Fail With Grace \(and Maybe Even Enjoy It\) -Say Yes More in Your Life -Get Out of Your Comfort Zone -Become a Better Public Speaker -Discover More About Yourself -...and have a blast doing it! Time and Dates : Tuesdays for eight \(8\) weeks, from March 24 to May 22, 7pm to 9:30pm NOTE: Attendance at the first session of your course is mandatory as it sets the agenda for the entire quarter. If you think you will have to miss the first class, please consider registering for the next quarter. No Prerequisites. All students begin at this level, even those with existing improv experience. It’s a prerequisite to the other levels. Location: 5700 6th Ave St, Suite 109, Umbrella Room, Seattle, WA 98108 \(In Georgetown, south of downtown, with plenty of parking.\) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: Ticket price $295.00 Age: 18 and above \*\*Cancellation policy: If you must cancel before the first class, we can only offer credit towards the same level class next quarter. We are unable to offer refunds. If you cancel after the first class we cannot offer credit towards a future class. Again, we are unable to offer refunds.

Event details

item.title: 2026 March Chamber Mixer and Small Business Resource Fair
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: DoubleTree Hotel, SeaTac, 18740 International Blvd, Seattle, WA

Chamber Mixer - March \+ Small Business Resource Fair Join us for our March Chamber Mixer bringing together local professionals, entrepreneurs, and community leaders for an evening of connection, collaboration, and valuable business resources. This month's mixer features a Small Business Resource Fair, where attendees can meet representatives from organizations offering tools, programs, and guidance to help small businesses grow and thrive. Whether you're looking to expand your network, explore partnership opportunities, or learn about business support services, this event provides a welcoming environment to make meaningful connections. Enjoy light refreshments, engage with fellow professionals, and discover new resources to support your business journey-all while taking in insights from the Port of Seattle about upcoming initiatives that impact our regional economy. Perfect for: ✔ Small business owners ✔ Startups and entrepreneurs ✔ Professionals looking to network ✔ Anyone interested in local business resources We look forward to seeing you there! BUSINESS INVOLVEMENT OPPORTUNITIES: Sponsorship opportunities are still available, for more information please download our Seattle Southside Chamber Event Sponsorship Form.

Event details

item.title: 2026 March Chamber Mixer Port of Seattle and Small Business Resource Fair
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: DoubleTree Hotel, SeaTac, WA

Chamber Mixer - March \(Port of Seattle\) \+ Small Business Resource Fair Join us for our March Chamber Mixer hosted in partnership with the Port of Seattle, bringing together local professionals, entrepreneurs, and community leaders for an evening of connection, collaboration, and valuable business resources. This month's mixer features a Small Business Resource Fair, where attendees can meet representatives from organizations offering tools, programs, and guidance to help small businesses grow and thrive. Whether you're looking to expand your network, explore partnership opportunities, or learn about business support services, this event provides a welcoming environment to make meaningful connections. Enjoy light refreshments, engage with fellow professionals, and discover new resources to support your business journey-all while taking in insights from the Port of Seattle about upcoming initiatives that impact our regional economy. Perfect for: ✔ Small business owners ✔ Startups and entrepreneurs ✔ Professionals looking to network ✔ Anyone interested in local business resources and Port of Seattle programs We look forward to seeing you there! BUSINESS INVOLVEMENT OPPORTUNITIES: Sponsorship opportunities are still available, for more information please download our Seattle Southside Chamber Event Sponsorship Form.

Event details

item.title: Understanding the Custom Home Process
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Learn how to build your dream home at our in-person Custom Homes 101 seminar, hosted inside a JayMarc model home. Free to attend!

Event details

item.title: Business After Hours: Virginia Mason Franciscan Health
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Description Feeling healthy? Let&'s find out at March&'s Business After Hours hosted by Virginia Mason Franciscan Health at their West Seattle location, where clinical care meets community focused service. VMFH is known for delivering high quality, patient centered healthcare while investing deeply in prevention, access, and partnerships that strengthen the neighborhoods they serve, including West Seattle. In person networking remains effective because face to face interaction improves trust, clarity, and follow up, a conclusion supported by Harvard Business Review research comparing in person and virtual business relationships. This evening offers space for thoughtful, healthy conversation and relationship building!

Event details

item.title: Business After Hours: VMFH
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Description Networking is a vital component of career success, with 80% of professionals considering it essential and nearly 100% believing that face-to-face meetings build stronger long-term relationships. Let&'s start 2026 out strong with some new connections!

Event details

item.title: HAPPY HOUR
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Description FREE Event for Lynnwood Chamber Members & their Guests - please register in advance.

item.title: Supporting Indigenous Communities Through Culture - Spring 2026
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Suquamish Clearwater Casino Resort, 15347 Suquamish Way Northeast, Suquamish, WA

Attendees will participate in a 4-day conference that will focus on HOC curriculum adaptation, implementation, and facilitation.

Event details

item.title: Living Large with Less Stuff
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Downsize to the right size! Take the stress out of de-cluttering with easy steps and practical advice for a successful transition. During this event, you will hear from the following speakers: Catherine Arendt, downsizing expert and Aljoya’s At Your Service Manager, works with families from start to finish as they transition from their homes to retirement communities. Nicole Bates, Relocation Consultant at Hansen Bros. Moving & Storage and HB Move Management, has assisted thousands of clients with their moves and specializes in working with clients downsizing from a long-time home to a smaller living space. Complimentary valet parking will be provided for your convenience.

Event details

item.title: Family Business Exchange | Webinar
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 12 PM

Save the Date! About the Family Business Exchange Seattle University’s Family Business Exchange \(FBX\) is a community of Pacific Northwest family business owners who gather to engage in education, networking, and professional development tailored specifically to their needs. FBX provides opportunities for families to address their unique challenges through relevant programming. It serves as a forum for information exchange with the goal of helping each member thrive through future generations. Areas of best practice that we cover include family business succession planning, governance, leadership development and growth, among others. About Assured Partners Assured Partners is a Seattle-based insurance brokerage that provides tailored commercial insurance, employee benefits, risk management and personal insurance solutions to businesses and individuals, drawing on the expertise of a national partnership of independent brokers. Please contact Nick Brewe to connect with Assured Partners about your business insurance needs. This event is open to FBX members and prospective members.

Event details

item.title: Electric SEA
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 12:01 PM

Please join us for Electric SEA 2026, an arts-oriented hackathon—part creative retreat, part pop-up lab, part public art salon.

item.title: Transform Your Greenery: A Creative Copper Plant Stick Workshop
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Amanda Long of Amanda Metals. Join us for an engaging and creative experience in this copper plant stick workshop! You will be creating three different copper and crystal glass bead plant sticks that will transform your greenery into stunning displays. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced creator, this workshop is designed to inspire your creativity. Let’s unleash your creative side and bring a touch of whimsy to your plants! All materials provided. $91 BBGS members/$130 nonmembers.

Event details

item.title: Electric SEA
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 12:01 PM

Please join us for Electric SEA 2026, an arts-oriented hackathon—part creative retreat, part pop-up lab, part public art salon.

Event details

item.title: Pilates for Gardeners
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Jo Anne Iaciofano. Grab your mat, kick off your shoes, and join this Pilates-inspired class to improve body movement, core strength, and muscle control. This beginner’s friendly class, using mind-body connection and breath, will take you through a series of exercises with an emphasis on technique and fluidity. We will focus on those areas most stressed during gardening by using your core to power the muscles increasing your energy and endurance. All levels are welcome. Wear comfortable clothing and be prepared to workout barefoot. Please bring a yoga or Pilates mat, towel, and water. This class will take place in the Aaron Education Center.  $17.50 BBGS Member/$25 Non-Member.

Event details

item.title: Mending Class: Sashiko Style Visible Mending
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Swoon City, 1716 Northwest Market Street, Seattle, WA

Join us for a chill mending class where we'll learn the art of Sashiko Style Visible Mending – no sewing experience required!

Event details

item.title: BiggerPockets Value-Add Conference
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Seattle, Wa, 98121, Seattle, WA

Real estate investors looking to sharpen their execution and scale for smarter deals will not want to miss the **first-ever BiggerPockets Value-Add Conference** in Seattle on March 28, 2026. This one-day, tight-knit event has only 120 seats available and is designed specifically for experienced investors actively executing deals. Led by seasoned local investor and Million Dollar Zombie Flips host James Dainard, alongside BiggerPockets experts Dave Meyer and Henry Washington, the conference offers a rare opportunity to learn how top investors scale deals in today’s competitive real estate market through stronger underwriting, strategic renovations, optimized financing, and disciplined execution systems. Investment to attend the one-day conference starts at $500, with a limited number of spots available HERE.  The in-person event features powerful speakers, delivering  practical, action-based strategies investors can implement immediately, including: Team building and legal structure for sustainable growth Underwriting and deal structuring across asset types Capital stack and financing strategies (hard money, DSCR, BRRRR) Construction and project management systems that protect margins Renovations and design decisions that directly impact ROI Scaling operations with stronger vendor management and streamlined processes This experience is designed for investors with three or more completed deals who are actively executing BRRRRs, flips, or small-scale development projects and looking to build repeatable, scalable systems. With Seattle’s competitive housing market and continued investor activity, the conference offers timely insight into how local operators are navigating tighter margins, rising costs, and evolving financing strategies. In addition to the conference, on March 29, a limited Premium Property Tour will take attendees inside active Seattle investment properties, where James and the speakers will walk through real deals and answer questions in small groups.

Event details

item.title: COUTURE MASTER SEWING CLASSES: (BEGINNER: Standard & Intensive)
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: 2265 116th Ave NE, 2265 116th Avenue Northeast, Bellevue, WA

Hands-on couture sewing class covering garment construction, fit, and finishing for beginners and creatives.

Event details

item.title: Hold Me Tight
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Hidden Valley Office Center, 1750 112th Avenue Northeast, Bellevue, WA

Join us for a workshop of connection and intimacy at Hold Me Tight, where we'll co-create a safe space to embrace vulnerability and strength

Event details

item.title: Master Your Camera Controls and Composition in the Field - Photo Workshops
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: BLANK, 2001 Western Avenue, Seattle, WA

Photography workshops lead by a professional photographer. Instructional sessions on camera controls followed by hands-on practice sessions.

Event details

item.title: Connect for Good: Sammamish Nonprofit Expo
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: SPC/Sammamish Presbyterian Church, 22522 NE Inglewood Hill Rd, Sammamish, WA

Connect for Good: Sammamish Nonprofit Expo 2026 Sponsored by: Puget Sound Energy, Sammamish Friends and Lead-ology Mark your calendar and join us for Connect for Good, a community-wide nonprofit expo designed to bring people, purpose, and impact together. On Saturday, March 28, from 10:00am–2:00pm, the Sammamish community is invited to gather at Sammamish Presbyterian Church for an inspiring afternoon of connection and service. Explore 30\+ local nonprofits, discover meaningful volunteer opportunities, and learn how you—and your family—can make a difference close to home. The event will feature youth leadership and student involvement opportunities, along with a Youth Zone & activities, making it welcoming and engaging for all ages. Whether you’re a student, family, community member, or volunteer, Connect for Good is the perfect place to support causes you care about, get involved in service, and strengthen our local community—all in one place. Hosted by the Sammamish Chamber of Commerce, in collaboration with local nonprofits and community partners.Nonprofit participation and sponsorship opportunities available.

Event details

item.title: Peninsula Birth, Baby, & Beyond Expo
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Kitsap Sun Pavilion, 1200 Fairgrounds Rd NW, Bremerton, WA

Come check out the Peninsula Birth, Baby & Beyond Expo for all things related to pregnancy, birth, and parenting in one convenient location!

Event details

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop [VIRTUAL]
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   Virtual workshops are held every 3rd and 4th Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. A Zoom link will be sent to you via a ULMS Housing department staff member upon completing registration.

Event details

item.title: Seed Starting Tips and Techniques
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Nancy Kartes and Bill Thorness. Our mild late winter allows us to sprout the edible garden early. Getting growing now will mean longer harvests and more use of our garden throughout the year. Learn what can be started now, whether to choose seeds or starts, where to site your early spring garden, how to know when the soil is ready, and how to plan ahead for the best success. Hands-on lessons will also teach you how to make a “seed snail,” how to use a soil block, and how to make your own seed-starting mix! You’ll also go home with some early-season seeds ready to sprout. $24.50 BBGS Member/$35 Non-Member.

Event details
